youtube-shorts.keyChanges: YouTube Shorts now prioritizes watch‑time and completion metrics over mere clicks, employs AI to surface trending formats, gives new creators a short‑term exposure boost if early engagement spikes, and refines thumbnails and titles automatically for higher relevance.
youtube-shorts.editingTips: Keep videos vertical and under 60 seconds, hook viewers in the first 3 seconds, use on‑screen captions, minimal transitions, and a clear call‑to‑action at the end.
youtube-shorts.postingTips: Post during peak U.S. morning hours (7‑10 am ET), aim for 3–4 Shorts per week, align with trending topics, and maintain a consistent schedule to build momentum.
youtube-shorts.titleTips: Include a keyword, create a hook, keep under 70 characters, and avoid clickbait while ensuring clarity.
youtube-shorts.descriptionTips: Add relevant keywords, a short CTA, up to 3–4 hashtags (#Shorts included), a link to your channel, and keep the description concise (≤100 words).

tiktok.keyChanges: Algorithm now prioritizes micro‑engagements, AI‑derived intent signals, and real‑time trend clustering.
tiktok.editingTips: Use 15‑second hooks, vertical framing, and dynamic captions; keep content under 60 s for higher completion rates; add trending sounds early; use on‑screen text to reinforce key points.
tiktok.postingTips: Post 3–4 times per day, spaced 3–5 hours apart; peak times are 9‑11 am and 6‑8 pm local time; experiment with early‑morning content for niche audiences.
tiktok.titleTips: Include a clear value statement, use emojis sparingly, and keep titles under 6 words; emphasize curiosity or benefit.
tiktok.descriptionTips: Add 1–2 relevant hashtags, use calls‑to‑action, and embed timestamps for longer videos; keep description concise and keyword‑rich.
summaries: AI predicts intent, not just views

instagram.keyChanges: Instagram’s 2026 algorithm prioritizes Reels and AI‑driven content curation, placing new videos at the top of feeds, enhancing story relevance through predictive engagement, and tightening privacy‑first data usage. The platform now rewards authenticity, consistent posting, and creator‑centric metrics such as completion rate and interaction velocity.
instagram.editingTips: Use vertical 9:16 formats, keep first 3 seconds hooky, add dynamic text overlays that match brand tone, apply color grading for emotional impact, compress files for fast loading, and leverage built‑in AI filters to reduce editing time.
instagram.postingTips: Post during peak user activity (7‑9 am and 8‑10 pm local time) and maintain a cadence of 2–3 posts per day for growth; post Stories daily for real‑time engagement and Reels at least once a day to tap into the feed‑first placement. Monitor analytics to adjust times and frequency.
instagram.titleTips: Create concise, keyword‑rich hooks; use emojis sparingly for personality; keep titles under 10 words; start with a question or bold statement; brand‑align the tone; end with a subtle call to action.
instagram.descriptionTips: Write 1–2 short paragraphs with a clear CTA; sprinkle brand‑relevant hashtags (5–10) in the first line; add line breaks for readability; tag collaborators and location; include a link in the bio or use the new link‑in‑bio feature for traffic.

youtube-long.keyChanges: YouTube’s 2026 algorithm now prioritizes long-form content that maintains high retention and engagement throughout the video. The recommendation engine uses content clustering and semantic relevance to surface longer videos, and early watch‑time signals (first 2–3 minutes) are amplified. Channels that consistently post series or episodic content receive a boost in discoverability, and new "long‑form tags" help the algorithm identify and promote videos over 10 minutes.
youtube-long.editingTips: Start with a 5‑second hook, use dynamic pacing, and incorporate engaging visuals or text overlays to keep viewers on track. Add chapters every 3–5 minutes and edit for smooth transitions to reduce drop‑off. Keep the first 30 seconds crisp, sprinkle key points throughout, and end with a strong call‑to‑action that encourages comments, likes, and shares.
youtube-long.postingTips: Publish during peak engagement times for your audience (typically 9 AM–12 PM or 6 PM–9 PM PST). Aim for 2–3 consistent uploads per week to maintain momentum without overloading viewers. Use scheduling tools to stagger releases evenly, and monitor watch‑time data to refine timing and frequency over time.
youtube-long.titleTips: Use clear, keyword‑rich titles that promise value. Keep it under 70 characters, include the primary keyword at the beginning, and add a subtle hook that hints at a benefit or solution. Avoid clickbait that misleads, as that hurts retention signals.
youtube-long.descriptionTips: Start with a concise summary of the video’s main points and a primary keyword. Add timestamps for chapters, a brief call‑to‑action, and links to related playlists or social media. Include a transcript snippet to improve SEO and accessibility, and use the first 1‑2 sentences as a compelling summary for search results.

facebook-reels.keyChanges: Algorithm now prioritizes completion rate, uses AI for content classification, emphasizes audio trends, integrates cross‑platform distribution, introduces Reel Boost for paid amplification.
facebook-reels.editingTips:
1. Capture interest in first 3 seconds.
2. Use trending audio and captions.
3. Keep videos 30–60 seconds.
4. Add subtitles for silent autoplay.
5. Use high‑contrast visuals and quick cuts.
6. Include brand logo or CTA in frame.
facebook-reels.postingTips:
Post 3–5 times per week.
Optimal times: 7‑9 am, 12‑1 pm, 6‑8 pm (local time).
Leverage Audience Insights to test days.
Use scheduling tools to maintain consistency.
Engage with comments within 30 minutes.
facebook-reels.titleTips: Use keyword‑rich, concise titles. Include emojis, keep under 40 characters. Add a call‑to‑action or tease the content.
facebook-reels.descriptionTips:
Include 3–5 relevant hashtags.
Mention collaborators or brands.
Add a short CTA or link.
Write within 200 characters.
Use line breaks for readability.
